title: Convert units multi-step word problems (US customary)
description: Solve word problems that involve converting between US customary measures of distance, volume, and mass.
topic_path: Converting US Customary units word problems
Convert units multi-step word problems (US customary)

assessment_items.question_content:
Gloria traveled a total of $8$ miles to get from school to her apartment.  Gloria took the bus from school and then walked the remaining $880$ yards home.  

**How  many *miles* did Gloria travel on the bus?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\blueE{880} \text{ yd}$ to miles.

$1\text{ yd} = \dfrac{1}{\maroonD{1760}} \text { mile}$ 

$\blueE{880}\text{ yd} = \dfrac{\blueE{880}}{\maroonD{1760}} =\tealE{\dfrac12}\text{ mile}$

Gloria walked $\tealE{\dfrac12}$ mile.

Now, we subtract the distance Gloria walked $\left(\tealE{\dfrac12} \text{ mile}\right)$ from the total distance she traveled $(\purpleD{8}\text{ miles})$.  



 

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\quad\quad\purpleD{8} - \tealE{\dfrac12}\\\\
&=\purpleD{7\dfrac22}-\tealE{\dfrac12}\\\\
&=7\dfrac12
\end{align}$
Gloria traveled $7\dfrac12$ miles on the bus.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A kangaroo hopped $\dfrac13$ of the distance to the lake with her baby in her pouch.  She hopped the remaining $7{,}040$ yards without her baby in her pouch. 

**How  many *miles* did the kangaroo hop to the lake?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
The kangaroo traveled $\maroonD{\dfrac13}$ of the total distance with her baby in her pouch.  So, she traveled $\blueE{\dfrac23}$ of the distance without her baby.    


[[☃ image 1]]

To find how far each $\maroonD{\dfrac13}$ is, we divide $7{,}040 \text{ yd}$ by $2$.  

$7{,}040 \div2= 3{,}520$



[[☃ image 1]]

Each third of the distance is $3{,}520 \text { yd}$.   How far is the entire distance?

$3{,}520 \text{ yd}  \times 3 = \purpleD{10{,}560} \text{ yd}$  



[[☃ image 1]]

      
The total distance to the lake was $\purpleD{10{,}560}$  yards.

$1 \text { yd} = \dfrac{1}{\tealE{1{,}760}}\text{ mile}$

$\begin{align}
\quad&\purpleD{10{,}560}\text { yd} \\\\
&=  \dfrac{\purpleD{10{,}560}}{\tealE{1{,}760}}\\\\
&=\purpleD{10{,}560}\div\tealE{1{,}760}\\\\
&=6\text { miles}
\end{align}$
The kangaroo hopped $6$ miles to the lake.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
A golfer took three shots on one hole. The distance of each shot are shown below:

* $167$ yards
* $49$ feet
* $7$ inches

**How many total *inches* did the ball travel?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] inches

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, let's convert $\blueE{167} \text{ yds}$ to inches.

$1\text{ yard} = 36 \text{ inches}$ 

$\begin{align}
&~\quad\blueE{167}\text{ yd}\\\\
& = \blueE{167}\times 36\text{ in}\\\\
&=\blueE{6{,}012}\text{ in}\\\\
\end{align}$

On the golfer's $\blueE{\text{first shot}}$, the ball went $\blueE{6{,}012}$ inches.

Now let's convert feet to inches:

$1\text{ foot} = 12 \text{ inches}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\maroonD{49}\text{ ft} \\\\
&= \maroonD{49}\times 12\\\\
&=\maroonD{588}\text{ in}
\end{align}$

On the golfer's $\maroonD{\text{second shot}}$, the ball went $\maroonD{588}$ inches.

To find the total distance the ball traveled, we add the distances.

$\blueE{6{,}012} +\maroonD{588} + \tealE7=6{,}607$
The ball traveled $6{,}607$ inches.

assessment_items.question_content:
A plumber had a pipe that was $2 \text { yards}$ long.  He cut $18 \text{ inches}$ off the end of the pipe.

**How  many *feet* long was the pipe after the plumber cut it?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] feet

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\blueE2 \text{ yd}$ to feet.

$1\text{ yard} = \tealE3 \text { feet}$ 

$\begin{align}
&\quad\blueE2\text{ yd} \\\\
&= \blueE2 \times \tealE{3}\\\\
&=\blueE6\text{ ft}
\end{align}$

The pipe was $\blueE6$ feet before the plumber cut it.

$1 \text { inch} = \dfrac{1}{\purpleD{12}}\text{ foot}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\maroonD{18}\text { in}\\\\
& =\dfrac{\maroonD{18}}{\purpleD{12}}\\\\
&=\dfrac32=\maroonD{1\dfrac12}\text { ft}
\end{align}$

The plumber cut $\maroonD{1\dfrac12}$ feet off the of the pipe.

We can subtract to find the new length of the pipe.

$\begin{align}
&\quad\quad\blueE6-\maroonD{1\dfrac12}\\\\
&=\blueE{5\dfrac22}-\maroonD{1\dfrac12}\\\\
&=4\dfrac12
\end{align}$
The pipe was $4\dfrac12 \text{ feet}$ long after the plumber cut it.

$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|

assessment_items.question_content:
Libby built a fence that was $56 \text { feet}$ long over $4$ days.  She built the same length of fence each day.  

**How  many *inches* of fence did Libby build each day?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] inches

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
Libby divided $\purpleD{56} \text{ ft}$ evenly over $\tealE4$ days.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{56}\text{ ft} \div \tealE4 = \maroonD{14} \text{ ft}$

Libby built $\maroonD{14} \text{ ft}$ each day.

We need to convert $\maroonD{14} \text{ ft}$ to inches.

$1\text{ foot} = \blueE{12} \text { inches}$ 

$\begin{align}
&\quad~\maroonD{14}\text{ ft} \\\\
&= \maroonD{14}\text{ ft} \times \blueE{12}\text{ in}\\\\
&=168\text{ in}
\end{align}$
Libby built $168 \text{ inches}$ each day.

- | | | |

$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|

assessment_items.question_content:
For his craft project, James bought $10$ pieces of ribbon that were each $2.1\text{ feet}$ long.    

**How many total *yards* of ribbon did James buy?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] yards

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
To find the total amount of feet James bought, we multiply.



[[☃ image 1]]



$\maroonD{2.1}\text{ ft} \times \tealE{10} = \purpleD{21} \text{ ft}$

James bought $\purpleD{21} \text{ ft}$ of ribbon.

We need to convert $\purpleD{21} \text{ ft}$ to yards.

$1\text{ foot} = \dfrac{1}{\blueE3} \text { yard}$ 

$\begin{align}
&\quad\purpleD{21}\text{ ft} \\\\
&= \dfrac{\purpleD{21}}{\blueE3}\\\\
&=\purpleD{21}\div\blueE3\\\\
&=\goldE7\text{ yd}
\end{align}$


James bought $\goldE7 \text{ yards}$ of ribbon.

assessment_items.question_content:
Randy made $10 \text{ quarts}$ of lemonade. He divided the lemonade equally between $8$ pitchers.  

**How  many *cups* of  lemonade did Randy put in each pitcher?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] cups

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\maroonD{10}\text{ qt}$ to cups.

$1 \text{ quart} = \tealE4\text{ cups}$

$\begin{align}
&~\quad\maroonD{10}\text{ qt}\\\\
&=\maroonD{10}\text{ qt}\times \tealE{4}\text{ c} \\\\
&= \blueE{40} \text{ c}
\end{align}$

Randy made $ \blueE{40}$ cups of lemonade.

Randy divided $ \blueE{40} \text{ c}$ into $\goldE8$ equal parts.


[[☃ image 1]]

$ \blueE{40}\text{ c } \div \goldE8 = \purpleD5 \text{ c}$
Randy put $5 \text{ cups}$ of lemonade in each pitcher.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Garcia family started the day with $2$ gallons of milk.  They drank $1$ quart of milk for breakfast and $3$ pints for lunch.  

**How  many *pints* of milk did the Garcia family have left after lunch?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] pints

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we let's convert $\tealE2\text{ gal}$ to pints.  

$1 \text{ gallon}=8\text{ pints}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\tealE2\text{ gal}\\\\
&=2\times 8\\\\
&=\tealE{16}\text{ pt}\\\\
\end{align}$

The Garcia family started with $\tealE{16}$ pints of milk.

Now, we can convert $\maroonD1$ quart of milk to pints.

$\maroonD1 \text{ quart}=\maroonD2\text{ pints}$

The Garcia family drank $\maroonD2$ pints of milk for breakfast.  They drank $\blueE3$ pints for lunch.

Now, we subtract.

$\tealE{16}\text{ pints}-\maroonD2\text{ pints}-\blueE3\text{ pints}=11\text{ pints}$
The Garcia family had $11$ pints of milk left after lunch.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ghalib loves making a special soda drink.  He fills $\dfrac13$ of the container with orange soda.  He fills the remaining space with $4$ cups of lemon-lime soda.

**How many *pints* of his special soda drink did Ghalib make?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] pints

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
After using up one third of the space in the container for orange soda, Ghalib filled  the remaining $\dfrac{2}{3}$ with $\tealE4$ cups of lemon-lime soda.  


[[☃ image 1]]

We can split $\tealE4$ cups into $2$ groups of $2\text{ c}$.

So each third of Ghalib's drink contains $2\text{ c}$ of soda.

[[☃ image 1]]

How many cups was the whole drink?
The whole drink was $2 \times 3 = \tealE6\text{ c}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We need to convert $\tealE6$ cups to pints. 
$1 \text{ cup}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE2}\text{ pints}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\tealE6 \text{ c}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\tealE6}{\blueE2}\\\\
&=\tealE6\div\blueE2\\\\
&=3\text{ pt}
\end{align}$

Ghalib made $3$ pints of his special soda drink.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jahlil made $14 \text{ pints}$ of ice cream.  He gave $1 \text{ quart}$ to his neighbor and then made milkshakes with half of the remaining ice cream.

**How  many *quarts* of ice cream did Jahlil use to make milkshakes?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] quarts

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\blueE{14}\text { pt}$ to quarts.  

$1 \text{ pint}=\dfrac{1}{\maroonD2}\text{ quarts}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\blueE{14} \text{ pt}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueE{14}}{\maroonD2}\\\\
&=\blueE{14}\div\maroonD2\\\\
&=\blueE7\text{ qt}
\end{align}$

Jahlil started with $\blueE7$ quarts of ice cream.

Now let's subtract the $\maroonE1$ quart he gave his neighbor. 

$\blueE7-\maroonE1=\purpleD6$

Jahlil had $\purpleD6$ quarts left and used half to make milkshakes.  What is half of $\purpleD6?$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD6\div\tealE2=\goldE3$
Jahlil used $3$ quarts of ice cream to make milkshakes.

assessment_items.question_content:
To make a sports drink for the football team, Jaylen filled $\dfrac9{10}$ of a large cooler with water.  Then, he filled the remaining space with $6$ cups of sports drink concentrate.

**How many *gallons* of sports drink did Jaylen make?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] gallons

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
The sports drink concentrate fills the remaining $\dfrac1{10}$ of space.  


[[☃ image 1]]



Each tenth of the cooler contains $6$ cups.

So, the entire cooler contains $6 \times 10 = \goldE{60} \text{ c}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE{60} \text{ cups} = {?} \text { gallons}$

$1 \text{ cup}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE{16}}\text { gallon}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\tealE{60} \text{ c}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\tealE{60}}{\blueE{16}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{15}{4}\\\\
&= 3\dfrac34\text { gal}
\end{align}$
Jaylen made $3\dfrac34$ gallons of sports drink.

assessment_items.question_content:
D'Naisa mixed her favorite shade of orange paint.  She had $1$ gallon of orange paint and added $2$ quarts of yellow paint and $3$ pints of red paint.

**How many *quarts* of paint did D'Naisa mix?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] quarts

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\tealE1\text{ gal}$ to quarts.  

$\tealE1 \text{ gal}=\tealE4\text{ qt}$

D'Naisa started with $\tealE{4}$ quarts of orange paint.

$1 \text{ pt}=\dfrac12\text{ qt}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\maroonD3 \text{ pt}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\maroonD3}{2}\\\\
&=\maroonD{1\dfrac12}\text{ qt}
\end{align}$

D'Naisa added $\maroonD{1\dfrac12}$ quarts of red paint.
She also added $\goldE2$ quarts of yellow paint.

Now, let's combine the amounts of orange, red, and yellow paint.

$\goldE4+\maroonD{1\dfrac12}+\goldE2=7\dfrac12$
D'Naisa mixed $7\dfrac12$ quarts of paint.

assessment_items.question_content:
Andrea picked $21$ pounds of peaches in her orchard.  She divided the peaches evenly between $4$ large baskets.
 
**How  many *ounces* of  peaches did Andrea put in each basket?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] ounces

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we need to convert $\tealE{21}\text { lb}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\maroonD{16}\text{ ounces}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\tealE{21}\text { lb}\\\\
&=\tealE{21}\times\maroonD{16}\\\\
&=\blueE{336}\text{ oz}
\end{align}$

Andrea picked $\blueE{336}\text{ oz}$ of peaches.

Andrea divided $\blueE{336} \text{ oz}$ into $\purpleD4$ equal parts.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{336}\div \ \purpleD4= \goldE{84}\text{ oz}$
Andrea put $84 \text{ ounces}$ of peaches in each basket.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jack bought $5 \text{ pounds}$ of Halloween candy.  He gave out $3\text{ pounds}$ of the candy to trick or treaters and ate $10\text{ ounces}$ of the candy.   

**How  many *ounces* of  Halloween candy does Jack have left?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] ounces

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
Jack bought $5$ pounds and gave out $3$ pounds.

$5-3=\maroonD2$

Jack had $\maroonD2$ pounds left and ate $\goldE{10}$ ounces.

To find out how much candy he now has, we need to convert the $\maroonD2\text { lb}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\blueE{16}\text{ ounces}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\maroonD2 \text{ lb}\\\\
&=\maroonD2\times\blueE{16}\\\\
&=\maroonD{32}\text{ oz}
\end{align}$

Jack had $\maroonD{32}\text{ oz}$ and ate $\goldE{10}\text{ oz}$.

$\maroonD{32}-\goldE{10}=22\text{ oz}$
Jack has ${22}\text{ ounces}$ of Halloween candy left.

assessment_items.question_content:
Twins Isaac and Isaiah were just born.  Isaac weighs $6$ pounds $2$ ounces and Isaiah weighs $5$ pounds $4$ ounces.  

**How  many *ounces* do Isaac and Isaiah weigh together?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] ounces

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, we can convert Isaac's weight, $\blueE6\text { lb } \blueE2 \text { oz}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

$\begin{align}
&~\quad\blueE6 \text{ lb}\\\\
&=\blueE6\times\purpleD{16}\\\\
&=96\text{ oz}
\end{align}$

Now, we add the $\maroonD2$ ounces:

$96 + \maroonD2 = 98$

Isaac weighs $98$ ounces.

Next, let's convert Isaiah's weight, $\tealE5\text { lb } \tealE4 \text { oz}$ to ounces.  

$1 \text{ pound}=\purpleD{16}\text{ ounces}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\tealE5 \text{ lb}\\\\
&=\tealE5\times\purpleD{16}\\\\
&=80\text{ oz}
\end{align}$

Now, we add the $\tealE4$ ounces:

$80 + \tealE4 = 84$

Isaiah weighs $84$ ounces.

Last, let's add their weights.

$98 + 84 = 182$
Isaac and Isaiah weigh $182$ ounces.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scientists have put together bones to make a T-Rex skull that has a mass of $600$ pounds.  They just found a new tooth fossil to add to the skull that has a mass of $40$ ounces.  
 
**How many *pounds* is the T-Rex skull with the new tooth?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] pounds

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
We need to convert $\tealE{40}\text { oz}$ to pounds.  

$1 \text{ ounce}=\dfrac{1}{\maroonD{16}}\text{ pound}$

$\begin{align}
&=\tealE{40}\text{ oz}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\tealE{40}}{\maroonD{16}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{2}\\\\
&=\blueE{2\dfrac12}\text{ lb}
\end{align}$

The tooth fossil has a mass of  $\blueE{2\dfrac12}\text{ lb}$.

We need to add the mass of the tooth to the mass of the existing skull.

$600 + \blueE{2\dfrac12}= 602\dfrac12$
The skull has a mass of $602\dfrac12$ pounds.

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
On Thursday, Movietown Theater made $\dfrac45$ of their popcorn with butter.  The remaining $35.2$ ounces were made without butter.   

**How many *pounds* of popcorn did Movietown Theater make on Thursday?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] pounds

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
The popcorn without butter makes up $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of the popcorn.  


[[☃ image 1]]

Each fifth is $35.2$ ounces.

So, Movietown made a total of $35.2 \times 5 = \purpleD{176}$ ounces of popcorn on Thursday.  


[[☃ image 1]]

We need to convert $\purpleD{176}\text{ oz}$ to pounds.
Movietown Theater made $11$ pounds of popcorn on Thursday.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mama Mia restaurant used $\dfrac12$ of their mozzarella cheese making pizza and the remaining $64$ ounces making pasta.  

**How many *pounds* of mozzarella cheese did Mama Mia start with?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] pounds

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
If $\dfrac12$ of the cheese was used on pizza, then the remaining portion of cheese used on pasta makes up the other half.


[[☃ image 1]]

Each half is $64$ ounces.  
So, Mama Mia started with  $64\times 2 = \purpleD{128}$ ounces of cheese.  


[[☃ image 1]]

We need to convert $\purpleD{128}\text{ oz}$ to pounds. 
$1 \text{ ounce}=\dfrac{1}{\tealE{16}}\text{ pound}$

$\begin{align}
&\quad\purpleD{128} \text{ oz}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\purpleD{128}}{\tealE{16}}\\\\
&=8\text{ lb}
\end{align}$
Mama Mia restaurant started with $8$ pounds of mozzarella cheese.
